item.title: 2022 SEO Tips : Load Speed and Images
item.dc:creator: aarontruax
item.content:encoded:
Images are a fundamental part of every website and allow visitors to develop an understanding of the company’s persona, they are the fastest & most effective way to connect a user to your brand. 

Although larger images hold the ability to tell a deeper story, with higher resolution comes larger files. Thus increasing website load times.

Who has patience for that? 

If your web page does not load rapidly, users may become frustrated and uninterested leading to loss of potential clients. 

In order to resolve this issue, it’s essential to optimize every image to reduce the file size without losing its quality. 

 

Benefits of implementing image optimization 

  • Improves page loading speed 

Although the website loading speed is dependent on many factors such as layout, design & images. Images consist of up to 65% of the website weight. Once optimized, there’ll be a big change in your loading speed. 

  • Helps boost in SEO ranking

Image optimization can be a powerful weapon in the SEO battlen. Google LOVES websites which take less time to load. Therefore, they’ll rank these websites higher in their search results.

  • Improved site user experience  

Overall user satisfaction and experience are critical for website return visitors.. If they’re able to locate and find the source of information they want within seconds it increases the chance of them using & recommending yoursite. 

  • Save server memory & reduce load on server :

Image optimization is also practical in saving server memory and reducing extra server load.

For example : 

Having two of the same images, one image size is 900kb and another image size is 340kb. Not only will the second image put lower weight on the server it’ll also save you bandwidth.

The goal of image optimization is to find the correct balance between minimizing file size and sufficient quality. Images are processed before uploaded to a server through photo editing programs. Additionally, there are multiple WordPress Plugins which can automatically compress images.

 

Choosing the Right Format 

There’s two factors to consider - choosing the right image file format & the amount of compression. The most common formats being : 

  • PNG : These are amazing for interactive documents such as webpages. They’re mainly used in web projects as you’re able to save your image with more colors and on a transparent background. This makes for a much sharper, web-quality image.
  • JPEG : This format is widely popular as it allows excellent optimization without loss of quality. 
  • GIF : GIFs are most common in their animated form. In their more basic form, GIFs are formed from up to 256 colors in the RGB colorspace. Due to the limited number of colors, the file size is drastically reduced.

Reducing the Photo Size 

Modern cameras & smartphones have the ability to take high resolution images. However, when creating your website it’s not necessary to upload images in full resolution, but to select the resolution which is required. 

The recommended resolutions are : 800 x 533px OR 1200 x 800px - and for iPhone retina display, we recommend 96pp exports for highest clarity.

 

Reducing File Size using Compression 

After selecting your desired image format and desired size, the images need to be compressed to reduce the file size. 

There are two types of compression methods : 

  • Lossy : The file size is reduced by eliminating data, this is not visible to the naked eye, but can significantly reduce file size. 
  • Lossless : This technique does not lose any data in the compression process. Lossless compression “packs” data into a smaller file size by using a kind of internal shorthand to signify redundant data.

The below chart tells the story of why being top dog is worth all the marbles - don't let images get in your way!
item.excerpt:encoded: Images are a fundamental part of every website and allow visitors to develop an understanding of the company’s persona, they are the fastest & most effective way to connect a user to your brand.

item.title: 5 Ways to Use Data To Transform Your Strategy
item.dc:creator: aarontruax
item.content:encoded:
Content Content Content

Content marketing isn’t an exact science, and the way people interact with content changes constantly. However, understanding content marketing analytics can help you make more informed decisions about your strategy, even if you don’t know how to use analytics.

So you want to learn about analytics but don’t know where to start? This article will tell you how to do that. Only 3% of bloggers never use analytics and 29% track every single one of their posts — meaning that most bloggers are either not using them at all or are not doing it correctly.

Who doesn’t love a good analytics tool? I know I do. And there’s no shortage of them out there. Google Analytics, HubSpot, ClickFunnels — all of these and more offer the results you need in order to gain insight and learn how to improve your content marketing strategy. But of course, a good analytics tool can only take you so far. You need the right strategy behind it and that’s precisely where I come in.

In this blog, we want to explain the importance of content marketing analytics, what they are and how they work. We'll also demonstrate how these analytics can be used to improve your strategy.

5 Ways to Use Content Marketing Analytics

Marketers have many options for data and analytics, but understanding the information and how to apply it to your strategy can be overwhelming. Here are nine ways to use content marketing analytics to your advantage.

1. Use Bounce Rate to See Which Pages Have the Highest Engagement and Identify Common Themes

A bounce rate is essentially the number of single-page visits as a percentage of visits. For example, if a person visits your site and bounces back within ten seconds, then they have a 100% bounce rate. Bounce rates vary greatly, depending on your website. For example, the average bounce rate for e-commerce sites is 47%. That means if one person visits the page and bounces back within ten seconds, then that page has had a 47% bounce rate. This can give you clues as to what pages might need improvement, or simply highlight the most engaging pages on your site. High bounce rates may not always be a bad thing—you should consider whether they indicate that visitors are engaging with a specific page or whether they're just going to one section of a site before exiting. From the left navigation in Google Analytics, go to Behavior > Site Content > Content Drilldown. To determine which pages have the highest bounce rate, click the “Bounce Rate” metric. A down arrow will appear, which indicates the metric is sorted from highest to lowest.

2. Our advice for using content marketing analytics to identify gaps and create new content is this:

The phrase “best of both worlds” gets tossed around frequently in our modern vernacular, as a way to describe a scenario where two very different concepts seem to be in stark contrast but, nevertheless, somehow coexist nicely.

We’ve never seen it more true than when I looked at the phrase “content marketing analytics.” These analytics tools look at both sides of the user journey: how visitors get to your site and what they do once they do. The “how” side of the journey is about identifying what people search for when they find you. This is an important matter for businesses of all sizes; 66% of businesses surveyed want to increase their content creation spending and we think that makes perfect sense because pages with consistent traffic but high bounce or exit rates are not meeting visitor needs. Thanks to keyword research, though, you can tell visitors what they specifically want and add content to those topics accordingly.

3. Compare Engagement Rates Across Platforms to Determine Mobile-Friendliness and Implement Changes

What devices are your website's visitors using? If you don't know, then your site may be under-serving a large number of your visitors. This not only drives traffic away but can harm your brand strategy in the process.

You might not know this, but worldwide internet traffic is broken down by device like so:

56.05% mobile

41.52% desktop

2.43% tablet

This breakdown differs depending on country, region, and website. However, one thing remains the same: a majority of people use a mobile device regularly. Even if your site is overall mobile-friendly, tools like Google Analytics can help you make it even better.

 

4. Use Heat Maps to Understand How Visitors Use Your Website so You Can Improve Flow and Optimize User Experience

The web is a beautiful place. It’s filled with colorful signs, banners, buttons and images. Over time, people have grown to recognize these things — we call it the web design language. But using just pictures to communicate the message of your website can be limiting. Tables, graphs, and flow charts provide useful information, but sometimes it’s more helpful to have visual data. There are a few tools that come in handy when understanding how visitors interact with the elements on your website: heat maps, scroll heatmaps, and click-heat maps.

Heat maps are visual representations of where visitors click on your webpage. Their goal is to understand what draws them in, what makes them scroll down the page, or where they don't even bother clicking on anything at all. The important thing about them is that they present the full picture of how users interact with your website and content. Tools like Google Analytics use heat maps to track visitors and optimize your content strategy. That's why it's important to not only track the "element most scanned" but also the amount of clicks and scrolls over an element or a whole page to learn even more about your customers' behaviors.

5. Understand Organic Search Metrics to Create Content Marketing Templates for Your Blog

The one thing you should always remember is that your blog is a living, breathing entity. Constant writing means new content and new insights. One of the best ways to get new ideas for your blog is to analyze the performance of content that you’ve already published. Understanding organic search metrics can help you create content marketing templates for your blog.

The one thing you should always remember is that your blog is a living, breathing entity. Constant writing means new content and new insights. One of the best ways to get new ideas for your blog is to analyze the performance of content that you’ve already published. Whether you’re reading blog posts or conducting research, seeing low bounce rates and high session duration on pages tells you that readers are enjoying what they see on your page and that it is likely ranking well on search engines (though this will change as Google evolves). This is because the first five pages of results account for two-thirds of all clicks from search engines. Remember these and other insights in order to create content marketing templates for your blog.
item.excerpt:encoded: Content marketing isn’t an exact science, and the way people interact with content changes constantly. However, understanding content marketing analytics can help you make more informed decisions about your strategy, even if you don’t know how to use analytics.

item.title: SEO Keyword Rankings: Choosing the Right Terms to Rank #1 on Google
item.dc:creator: aarontruax
item.content:encoded:
More traffic means you’ll boost your business revenue, right? 

Not necessarily.

Although an increase in traffic is beneficial, there’s no guarantee it’ll lead to a revenue increase. Drawing the wrong traffic could stall your revenue due to your content being presented to the wrong audience. 

Alternatively, concentrate on receiving better quality traffic. One way of doing this is prioritizing ranking well for high internet keywords. 

If you look at a Google search result, you’ll notice that the top results receive the most clicks. The first results get 31.7% CTR and is 10 times more popular than the rest. 

Where would you rather be if you’re trying to grow your business, spot one or ten?

Exactly.

Taking this into consideration, here’s how keyword ranking for SEO works and how to choose the correct keywords for your content. 

What Is Keyword Ranking in SEO?

You’ve probably heard the word “keyword” in SEO. Here’s a quick breakdown: A keyword is a singular word or phrase you enter into a search engine. Ideally, the search engine will show results that match what you’re looking for.  Your “keyword ranking” indicates to your position the search results for a given search query. 

Here are some basics about keywords:

  • The average keywords draws in approximately 989 searchers per month.
  • About 14.1% of keyword searched involve a question
  • Keywords differ in length but the average is 1.9 words long. 

For example, you sell electric hand mixers. Within your product category page you’ll need to include keywords which people may enter into Google to find your product. In this case, words such as: “buy electric hand mixer” 

Remember, your keyword ranking is the spot your page occupies in the list of Google search results. 

SEO, or “search engine optimization,” is a process of optimizing your content to improve your page ranking. Let’s go over why it’s so important! 

Why Are SEO Keyword Rankings Important?

Organic search queries drive most website traffic. At least 53% of all website traffic comes from organic search queries.

By advancing your SEO keyword rankings, your website traffic will elevate. 

If you want to boost your brand, it is a priority to have traffic that converts. 

To snag the number one stop you must be strategic with the keywords you choose and how you’re using them in your digital marketing campaigns. 

Data Points to Consider when Selecting SEO Keywords

The process of choosing suitable keywords is difficult, however here are some important data point to consider before choosing your keywords: 

Search Volume  

Search volume is one of the most important factors to consider if you’re looking to buy SEO traffic. 

It reveals the popularity and competitiveness of a keyword, which will play a big role in your campaign whether you’re considering high or low-volume keywords. 

Find the right balance of keywords to optimize, and you’ll have a more successful campaign that doesn’t cannibalize your other traffic sources.

CPC 

The Cost-per-click is the amount you pay when someone clicks on paid ads. 

You can see if high CPC terms are converting especially well or if your own organic traffic might convert low by looking at the CPC. Average CPC varies by industry but is often around $1.40 a click for travel companies and $8.67 a click for lawyers.

For some industries, it’s helpful to learn more about the average CPC range by comparing your competition.

If you do plan to invest in paid ads, remember : 

  • High birds might not see great ROI 
  • Low bids might not receive enough exposure for your ads 

SEO Difficulty 

SEO difficulty scores reflect how challenging it is to rank for a certain keyword.

A low SEO difficulty results in easier ranking for the keyword, but this could potentially mean there’s a low search volume.

A high SEO difficulty score means there’s heavy competition for the keyword. You might find it difficult to rank against larger competitors. By creating greater quality content, you’ll  outrank your competitors. 

Paid Difficulty 

This refers to how easy or difficult it is to rank for a keyword in paid search results.

A high difficulty means you’ll pay more to rank for the key word, whereas low difficult keywords are easier to rank, therefore cost less. However, they may not be worth paying for. 

How to Choose Keywords That Can Get You the Top Spot in Google Keyword Rankings

1. Research and Compile a List of Topics to Cover

When researching your audience’s questions, ask yourself what topics they care about. Look for common questions across several platforms to find popular topics that are in demand.

Platforms like Reddit, Quora and Answer the Public. Reddit has over 50 million daily users, therefore it’s a good starting point for researching questions. 

Once you have a handful of topics in mind, create a list of potential questions to answer and keywords for these questions that you can use later in your content.

2. Plug Your Target Topics Into a Tool Like Ubersuggest

Research what topics your audience cares about and then answer their questions. 

Tools like Ubersuggest can help you find lucrative keywords so you know where to focus your SEO efforts. Once you have content up and running, perform routine SEO audits to identify problems.

Ubersuggest is simple and easy to use, just type the phrase in and click search! 

You’ll be able to identify how popular keywords are, in addition it’ll provide recommendations as to related keywords. 

3. Drill Down Into the Data to Find the Best Keywords for Your Topics

How do you find the right keywords for your topics?

Again, Ubersuggest is perfect for this! The “Keyword Overview” section reveals your chosen keyword’s search volume, CPC and difficult score. 

By clicking “Add to List” you’re able to add keywords into your research list. Furthermore, by clicking “Keywords Ideas” Ubersuggest will suggest related keywords. 

Remember, there are no “right” keywords; however, you might opt for a mixture of types such as questions, short phrases and comparisons in order to boost variety, this is a great starting point.  

 

Bonus Keyword Ranking SEO Tips and Best Practices

Here are five practical tips for boosting your Google keyword rankings in order to maximize your shot at being ranked #1. 

1. Optimize for Featured Snippets

According to the research of SEMRush and Brado, at least 19% of search engine result pages (SERPs) have a featured snippet. They have taken the top spot almost 100% of the time. This means, as featured snippets become more popular, they’re changing search results.

The most important thing is to pick a keyword with great long-tail intent. Ideally, a long-tail keyword would be a question or request that people really want answers to, like “What is the best baby carrier?”. If you write an authoritative answer to that question, it’s very likely that you’ll get a featured snippet on their SERP. You can use tools like Ubersuggest to find long-tailed keywords.

2. Use the Primary Keyword Strategically

Picking your primary keyword is one of the most important decisions. Getting this right can make the difference between releasing a product that not only meets goals but exceeds expectations.

One of the best ways to use this primary keyword is in the page title. To optimize your SEO and improve the overall effectiveness of your product, make sure you use the keyword in your title, make it memorable, and try to include it often in the rest of your page.

Be careful not to stuff keywords in your content, as excessive use could damage your SEO and affect your rankings.

3. Optimize Meta Descriptions

A meta description of a page summarizes its content. When optimized, it explains what your page is about, which could potentially generate better-targeted traffic.

How to optimize your own meta descriptions 

  • Use your primary keyword in the meta 
  • Keep your meta description under 160 characters 
  • Include a CTA when trying to sell the contents of the linked page

4. Streamline Your Page Speed

A page that takes more than four seconds to load will have low conversion rates, according to Portent’s research. Bouncing from a site after five seconds will cause a drop of 4.42 percent per second, and 78 percent of websites have a load time of five seconds or less. A slow loading time can also damage your SEO.

You can optimize your page performance, but keep in mind that optimizing every part of your website may have unintended side effects.

PageSpeed Insights helps to analyze and test current page speeds, with just one simple input of your URL. 

Compressing images, deleting unnecessary plugins and caching pages to reduce load time are adjustments you can make in order to optimize your website's speed.

5. Use Anchor Text With Keywords

Anchor text is a feature which takes the reader from one page to another. It should provide context as to what the linked page includes. 

Google recommends using no more than a few words for anchor texts. Around 50% of your inner-page clickable text should contain keyword variations.

Using a variation of keywords within for inner pages will help to avoid keyword stuffing and compromising readability by forcing your keywords to match exactly. URL anchors should be short and concise. 

 

Conclusion: SEO Keyword Rankings

Prioritize your keyword rankings for SEO. No one wants to be on page three just because they choose the wrong keywords. 

Give the suggested SEO tools a go!
item.excerpt:encoded: Picking your primary keyword is one of the most important decisions. Getting this right can make the difference between releasing a product that not only meets goals but exceeds expectations.

item.title: An Introduction to Integrated Digital Marketing
item.dc:creator: aarontruax
item.content:encoded:
Integrated Digital Marketing 

As the digital world evolves, so do the landscape of online marketing. In the past, it was all about gaming the system with keywords stuffing, sketchy backlinks, and bamboozling search engine algorithms. Now, there are a multitude of factors that go into determining your search engine ranking, it's a constant fight to stay ahead of the competition.

Investing in a comprehensive digital marketing strategy is crucial to stay afloat in today's climate. But with so many options and moving parts, it can be difficult to know where to start or what will give you the biggest return on your investment.

It's important to choose your battles carefully and invest in marketing strategies that have a high chance of success. With careful planning and execution, you can give yourself an edge over the competition.

How exactly do you gather the intel required for this?

Integrated Digital Marketing.

What is Integrated Digital Marketing? 

Integrated Digital Marketing is a digital marketing strategy that employs multiple approaches and technologies with the aim of creating a consistent brand image across all channels.

It encompasses anything digital that promotes your brand, such as your website, articles, social media widgets, and call-to-actions. This strategy also takes into account search engine optimization to draw users from Google searches.

The goal of integrated digital marketing is to focus heavily on linking all online presences together instead of keeping them separate.

Each social media account should link to multiple other social media platforms in addition to your website. Your website content should link to service pages to help guide customers while also improving SEO.

Why is Integrated Digital Marketing Important? 

There are many factors that influence a customer's decision to purchase a product. One of the most important is exposure - the more times a potential customer sees your product, the more likely they are to buy it. This is why it's so important to have multiple touchpoints, or ways for customers to see your product. 

For example, if someone sees your product on social media, searches for it on Google, and then sees it in ads on YouTube, they're much more likely to buy it than if they only saw it once.

Word-of-mouth is also a powerful marketing tool. When potential customers hear about your product from trusted sources, they're more likely to be interested in buying it.

If you want to grow your brand and increase sales, you need to make sure you're exposing your product to as many people as possible through a variety of channels.

Making Use of Analytics

Digital marketing gives the ability to track data and analytics. With more systems in place, you can gather a lot of information that can be used to make decisions. The right technology can help you see where your online traffic is coming from. You can then use this data to improve the channels that are not performing well. Alternatively, you can invest more into the channels that are doing well. Additionally, this data allows you to adjust your advertising to appeal to popular demographics from each marketing channel. In other words, you can see how effective your digital marketing strategies are and if they are worth your investment.

Want to explore Integrated Digital Marketing?
Reach out to us at hello@truaxmarketing.com and stay tuned for our next blog!
item.excerpt:encoded: The digital world is continuously advancing, with mindful planning and execution, you can give yourself an edge over the competition with Integrated Digital Marketing.

item.title: Benchmarking Your LinkedIn Ads
item.dc:creator: aarontruax
item.content:encoded:
How well are your LinkedIn Ads performing?

LinkedIn offers many different types of ads, each with their own benchmarks for success. 

Ad content on LinkedIn can be divided into three categories: Sponsored Content, Text & Dynamic Ads, and Sponsored Messaging. While each format possesses its own advantages, figuring out which one works best for your business requires taking a closer look at key metrics such as click-through-rate, cost-per-click, and conversion rate.

Sponsored Content

LinkedIn offers a number of different ad types that can be displayed in users' news feeds, including Sponsored Content. 

Sponsored Content has an average click-through rate (CTR) of 0.44%, so your ads' performance is determined by how much higher your CTR is than that benchmark. 

Costs-per-click (CPC) for Sponsored Content usually falls between $8 and $11, but it can increase if you're targeting a small audience or one that is in high demand. In those cases, CPCs could be as high as $15-$20. 

For most audiences you should aim for a CPC of $8-$11 to be cost efficient. If you're generating clicks for less than that, you're in good shape.

The conversion rates of your Sponsored Content Ads will depend on how difficult it is for customers to take advantage of the offer. If the offer is low-friction, such as simply downloading a piece of content or watching a video in exchange for contact information, you can expect a conversion rate of 10% to 15%. However, if the offer is high-friction, like requiring a demo or free trial, the conversion rate will usually be only 1.5% to 4%.

Text & Dynamic Ads 

LinkedIn offers two different types of right-rail ads: Text Ads and Dynamic Ads. Text Ads are the cheaper option, with manual bidding options as low as $2 per click or per thousand impressions. Most advertisers see a CPC of $3 to $6 with Text Ads. Dynamic Ads take up more space in the right rail and typically have a higher CTR, around 0.08%. Because they don’t typically drive as much traffic as other ad types, bidding much more than this range likely won’t be very cost effective.

LinkedIn's Text Ads are a small banner ad that only appears on desktop devices. With an average CTR of only 0.025%, these ads can easily be missed by users. However, because they take up less space than other types of ads, they can be a more cost-effective option for advertisers.

Sponsored Messaging 

LinkedIn's Sponsored Messages are a great way to get your message in front of users' eyeballs. With an average open rate of 55%, you're sure to reach your target audience. Plus, with a click-through rate (CTR) of 3.2%, you can be confident that your message is being seen and interacted with.Sponsored Messaging is the most expensive ad format on LinkedIn, with CPCs (cost per click) typically ranging from $25 to $60.

Most people find message ads to be quite invasive. After all, you're interrupting someone's private communication in order to deliver your commercial message. So, if you want people to actually pay attention to your ad, it's important to make it feel like a personal invitation rather than a generic sales pitch.

It's no secret that exclusive events and limited time deals generate a lot of interest. In fact, our experience has shown that they can result in open rates of 70% and CTRs of 7%. This kind of engagement can help lower your costs into a range closer to that of Sponsored Content.

Looking for a LinkedIn ad strategy that generates high quality leads?

Let’s connect: hello@truaxmarketing.com
item.excerpt:encoded: Perfecting your LinkedIn Ad strategy is crucial for a successful marketing strategy.

item.title: The Magic of Fractional CMOs
item.dc:creator: aarontruax
item.content:encoded:
The best addition to your workplace since casual Fridays.

What is a Fractional CMO?

Welcome to the exciting world of Fractional CMOs! 

In a nutshell, Fractional CMOs are experienced marketing leaders who are hired to be part-time executive additions to your company. Hiring a fractional CMO is a great option for anyone who’s not quite ready to hire a full time CMO but doesn’t want to outsource marketing leadership to an agency or consultant.

Fractional CMOs can help build your strategic objectives and achieve next-level growth and profitability - regardless of what products or services you’re marketing. Your business will thank you! 

What does a Fractional CMO do?

A fractional CMO works with you to develop strategic insights, formulate branding strategy, and oversee your company’s marketing transformation. They become embedded within your leadership team and gain a deep understanding of your business’ objectives. With this knowledge, they’re able to develop fresh and impactful marketing strategies that drive growth for your organization.

With years of marketing and managerial experience, Fractional CMOs are c-suite level strategists that help organizations meet and exceed their marketing goals. 

At Truax Marketing Solutions, we believe your Fractional CMO should work closely with both your organization's leadership team and your marketing team.. Your Fractional CMO is responsible for: 

🔹Developing both short-term and long-term marketing strategies. 

🔹Deciding which marketing strategies are best for your business and helping you commit to them. This includes SEO, content marketing, paid advertising, podcasts, etc.

🔹Quickly and thoughtfully solving any marketing problems that arise. 

🔹Creating fresh and engaging content that boosts you above your competitors.

🔹Leading the marketing department and inspiring your team.

What are the benefits of hiring a Fractional CMO?

Fractional CMOs are a great way for companies to get access to the skills and insights of experienced marketing executives without having to pay for a full-time employee. This allows companies to find the right mix of experience that fits both the culture of their organization and their budget.

Hiring a full-time CMO means your growth is dependent on one person's experience and expertise. With Fractional CMOs however,their work with multiple companies means they have tons more experience and are more likely to succeed.

Involving a fractional CMO in your marketing efforts allows you to benefit from their vast experience and skill set to create a winning marketing strategy. A good fractional CMO will use their knowledge from multiple industries to build a customized strategy for your business that sets you up for success.

Signs you need a Fractional CMO

🔹Marketing strategy: Your business needs a comprehensive marketing strategy for your niche and industry.

🔹Experience: Your team lacks strategic experience. You need someone who has seen it all and can anticipate challenges and obstacles.

🔹Reporting and KPIs: You need to monitor the health and progress of your campaigns and have someone lead weekly KPI meetings. 

🔹Marketing Leadership:  Your marketing team requires leadership from an experienced marketing professional that can help in the decision-making process.

Looking to hire a Fractional CMO?

Here at Truax Marketing Solutions we’re here to help. Our team of dedicated and passionate fractional CMOs are excited to help you develop a killer marketing strategy and take your organization to the next level. Genius advice starts as low as $300/hr. 

Contact us today at hello@truaxmarketing.com and get cracking on your marketing dream team!
item.excerpt:encoded: Every business needs a little help with their marketing strategy, and Fractional CMOs are here to help!

item.title: How to Build AWESOME Backlinks
item.dc:creator: aarontruax
item.content:encoded:
Search engine optimization, or SEO, is essential for driving traffic to your website and boosting your position in Google search results. Backlinks are the perfect way to crank up the SEO factor and get your name on the board. If you're looking to grab some quality backlinks and improve your site's Google ranking, keep reading.

Here are 9 of the hottest link-building strategies right now. 

1. Use Data to Get Links From News Sites

If you’re new to the world of backlinks, it can be tricky to get the hang of at first. Luckily, there are platforms that help connect sources with journalists, like HARO. HARO is a great way to make connections and get set up with the right people to share your content. 

This platform connects you not only with journalists who are planning features in advance, but reporters that are looking for breaking news stories. If there is breaking news related to your niche, this is a great opportunity to pick up mentions and links. Here’s what to do.

🔹Set up a Google alert of BuzzSumo alert

🔹Reach out to reporter with added value

If you want to get a link from a news site, you need to be willing to put in some effort. You have to offer something of value to the reporter. Making their life easier will get you major brownie points.

Reaching out and trying to piggyback off of their work by simply saying "I see you wrote about this, I wrote about it too" will only get you flagged as a spammer.

How can you provide value?

🔹 Offer unique data or statistics

🔹 Offer an alternative take

🔹 Create an infographic or custom visual they can embed in their story 

🔹 Give them a quote with personal insight from your industry. Include your credentials

🔹ACT FAST - the news is always changing, so you need to be quick to get the story. 

Today's newspaper is tomorrow's recycling, so it's important to reach out as soon as you see a story breaking. Pro tip. Create a personal black book of journalists who cover your industry and, ping them immediately when you see a story developing. That way, you might catch them while they're still working on an article for publication.

2. The Skyscraper Technique

If you're looking for a super simple way to get more traffic to your website, look no further than this 3-step approach.

🔹Find a popular piece of content in your niche

🔹Create something even better

🔹Promote it like crazy

With just a little bit of effort, you can see some amazing results.

There are a few different ways that you can go about finding the most popular keywords in your niche. One way is to simply search for a popular keyword on Google and see which page is ranking at #1. Most likely, that page will have backlinks powering that ranking.

However, if you're not sure which topic is going to be best for attracting links, there is a way to take the guesswork out of it. Semrush and other link analysis tools have a report called "Best by links". This report will show you the most linked-to pages on any given site.

For example, if you look at my Google Ranking Factors post, you can see that it has picked up links from almost 5,000 domains!

3. The Moving Man Method 2.0

If you're looking for a way to snag some quality links from outdated content, the Moving Man Method 2.0 is a great option. 

This time, instead of focusing on businesses that have gone pop or rebranded, we'll leverage outdated information. Why? Because things change!

For example, let's say we had a page that ranked the most popular websites in the world. At one point, YouTube overtook Facebook as the second most visited website on the planet. However, over time things have changed and now there are a ton of pages that contain outdated information.11,000 to be precise! 

You can…

🔹 Reach out to all the sites with old information

🔹 Let them know it’s changed

🔹 Point them to our own page while we’re there

4. Double Down on Effective Content Formats

When it comes to creating content that will attract quality links, try to avoid relying on guesswork.

There are a number of proven content formats that people love to link to, such as: 

🔹Quizzes

🔹”Why” Posts

🔹”How to” Posts

🔹Infographics

🔹Videos

🔹List Posts 

Try out a few different formats and see what works best for you.

5. Podcast Link Building

Podcasts are also a great way to build backlinks.

To find podcasts to go on, try searching for headshots of people in your niche on Google reverse image search. 

This should give you a good idea of which podcasts are popular in your field, and who the key players are.

6. Resource Page Link Building

A resource page is a great way to link to other useful pages on the web. 

As a link builder, you can use a resource page to find potential link opportunities and reach out to potential partners. 

To create a successful resource page, be sure to have something that is worthy of a link and write a unique version that will stand out from the rest.

Pro Tip: Try reaching out to OTHER resource pages to suggest they link to YOUR resource page!

7. Unlinked Brand Mentions

If you see your brand mentioned in an article without a link, that's a great opportunity to reach out and ask for one. In most cases, the publication will be happy to oblige.

You can set up an alert for your brand name so you'll be notified any time it's mentioned online. This way, you can make sure you're always taking advantage of opportunities to promote your business.

8. Name Your Strategies

🔹 Figure out a process or strategy that’s unique to your business.

🔹 Give it a creative and catchy name

🔹 Write about it 

Writing about the process is vital. You’ll only get links if…

🔹 Your strategy is extremely useful

🔹 You can prove it works

Pro Tip : Always feature real-life case studies when revealing a new strategy or technique 

9. Two-Step Email Outreach

Reaching out to potential link partners before you launch a new post can often yield better results than simply asking for a link right away. 

Try this approach: Reach out to someone you think would be interested in your upcoming article and give them a brief summary of what it's about. Ask them if they would be open to receiving the full article once it's published. In most cases, the answer will be a resounding yes! 

A little courtesy goes a long way in outreach, and many journalists will appreciate the time you take to provide them with quality content that is relevant to them.

If you still need some help grabbing high quality backlinks, we’ve got you covered. 

Let’s connect! Click here to start and we’ll be excited to help you create some killer backlinks.
item.excerpt:encoded: Looking to improve your site's Google ranking? Backlinks are a great way to do that. Learn how to get quality backlinks and make your site more visible.

item.title: The Magic of Video Marketing
item.dc:creator: aarontruax
item.content:encoded:
What video marketing is and why you need to be using it

Video marketing is an essential tool for any business looking to promote themselves online. 

By creating engaging and informative videos businesses can reach a wider audience and build brand awareness. 

Platforms such as Youtube, Facebook, Snapchat and Instagram are ideal for video marketing, as they allow businesses to share their content with a global audience.

Why is Video Marketing important? 

Video has quickly become one of the most popular types of content on the internet.

It’s a great way to show off your product or service and helps to increase engagement on social media channels. It educates your audience and allows you to reach them through a new medium. 

Now more than ever consumers are using video in order to make decisions about what products to buy and where to buy them from. It was found that 72% of consumers prefer watching a video about a product over reading a description of that product (Hubspot)

Taking advantage of this channel means more engagement and higher conversions for your product. Over 80% of businesses now use video marketing to promote their product or service.(WyzOwl) 

How do You Develop a Video Marketing Strategy?

Developing a solid strategy is key to successful video marketing. 

Your marketing strategy should include…

🔹Ample research on your customers pain points 

🔹Developed solutions to your customers problems 

🔹A unique look and feel for your brands videos 

🔹A plan to integrate your videos into your text-based marketing content 

🔹A way to measure the results of your videos 

🔹A solid outline and script for your video content

🔹A plan to improve your content strategy based on feedback and data from your target audience

🔹Distributing your videos on social media (IG, FB) and dedicated video platforms (Youtube)

Video Templates

1 : How-to Video

How-to videos are a great way to learn something new. Whether you want to bake a cake or finally figure out how to do a pushup, how-to-videos are an easy to follow and fun way to learn new skills. 

For businesses, how-to videos can be extremely helpful to expose your brand to potential customers.  Showing people how to use your product or service is a great way to improve conversions and bring people to your site.

2 : Product Demo

Product demos are another way to increase conversion and build brand awareness, but they can be a tricky medium to master. Many product demos make the mistake of starting off by trying to show how great their product is. But this is a huge mistake.

While it's fine to show your product briefly at the beginning of the video, the real goal should be to introduce the problem that your product solves - and do so quickly.

3 : Explainer Video

Here’s where you explain a tricky concept tied to your product or service. This serves as a great way to expose your product to viewers in a more organic way. It provides your product as a practical solution for whatever you’re explaining. 

4 : The Case Study

Customer testimonials are one of the most powerful tools you can use to increase conversion rates. Seeing a real person talking about how great a product or company  is can have a much bigger impact than text alone.

Equipment

You don't need expensive equipment to make high-quality videos. In fact, you can usually get all the equipment you need for a few hundred dollars. If you're on a tight budget, we’ll show you a few hacks you can use to make pro videos without breaking the bank. 

Although investing in a good quality camera will be beneficial if you’re looking to create video content long-term, using an iPhone camera works just fine. 

Make sure you’re in a bright room with natural lighting for the best results. If your filming location does not provide this, investing in a ring light or softbox is a great idea. After all, you can never have enough lighting.

Depending on where you’re shooting and what you’re shooting, you may even need a few different kinds of lighting. It’s essential to produce a well lit video to make the viewing experience as enjoyable for your audience as possible.

You’re ready to go! With your amazing product and new skill set, you’re ready to get rolling and show the world what you got. 

Struggling to make a video that does your product justice? We’re here to help. 

Let’s connect!
item.excerpt:encoded: Video marketing is essential for growing businesses. Create engaging content and build brand awareness with videos!

item.title: 10 Essential SEO Skills You Need to Know in 2023
item.dc:creator: aarontruax
item.content:encoded:
What is the greatest skill in SEO? 

The list is endless, but we can never say that there is one greatest skill. 

Why? 

Because SEO requires a combination of hard skills and soft skills. 

Here are 10 essential SEO skills you need in order to be successful in marketing. 

Critical Thinking

As an SEO professional, one of the most important skills you can develop is critical thinking. 

With so much noise and conflicting information out there, it can be all too easy to simply accept claims and advice at face value, without giving them a second thought.

However, this often leads to disappointing results, as what works for one situation may not work for another. 

SEO pros should be able to look at data and understand the “3 what’s”

🔹 What happened

🔹 Why “what happened” happened

🔹 What should be done about it 

SEO’s who engage in critical thinking read studies, analyze the data and outcomes, and seek out other studies that may contradict the results. 

By doing this, they can form their own opinions and develop strategies that are tailored to the specific client scenario. 

Experimenting

The ability to experiment is critical for success in SEO. 

What works for one business or industry may not work for another, so it’s important to test different approaches to see what produces the best results. 

Additionally, every project has a different audience, so it’s necessary to constantly adapt your SEO strategy to ensure that you’re reaching the people who are most likely to convert.

Test out different types of content, such as videos, reels, and carousels. Adjust your strategy to what works best for you and your audience.

Problem Solving

The ability to solve problems and see the big picture is essential for success in SEO.

We often get so caught up in the details of various SEO tasks and we miss the overall picture of what is happening.

It can be helpful to take a step back and look at things from end to end in order to solve problems more effectively. This means considering things like the overall goal of the website, how search engines work, and what potential visitors are hoping to find. By taking a holistic approach, we can ensure that our SEO efforts are more successful.

Adaptability

The ever-changing landscape of search engine optimization can be a difficult one to navigate. 

With new algorithms and updates constantly being rolled out by Google, it can be hard to keep with the latest best practices. However, the one constant in the world of SEO is change itself. Those who are able to adapt to the constantly changing landscape will not only survive, but thrive. 

While it is important to have a solid understanding of all the aspects of SEO, from technical skills to content creation, the ability to adapt is what will set you apart from the competition.

In an agency setting, where new clients and projects are always coming in, being able to adjust on the fly is essential. 

Communication

As a SEO pro, a big part of being successful is the ability to communicate clearly and concisely with clients and colleagues. This involves being able to explain concepts and objectives in many different ways, depending on the person's level of understanding.

The Ability to Learn

SEO is all about learning. Constantly being able to learn new things and adapt to new ways is the greatest skill you can have in this field. Things are always changing in SEO, so being able to keep up is essential.

Persistence

Persistence is the key to success. No matter how many times you fail, you must never give up. Always learn from your failures and strive to improve. Engage with other teams and cooperate for the greater success of the business you represent.

Inquisitiveness

A curious mind is essential for success in SEO. You might be technically gifted and brilliant, or a content marketing expert, but without curiosity, you won't be able to see a project from all sides and make the most of its potential.

Understanding the User

The most important thing for an SEO to understand is the "information need" of an end user. They don't start their research with any predetermined labels like "consumer" or "B2C customer" or "B2B". They're just a human being trying to solve a problem.

Research & Troubleshooting

To be a successful SEO professional, you need to have strong writing skills and be able to research well. However, for technical SEO, the most important skills branch out into different areas.

It is important to understand what users want and how they search for it. Data interpretation is key to uncovering these insights. To be truly successful, you must be able to analyze data and use it to inform your decisions.

This requires both quantitative and qualitative skills. You must be able to understand complex data sets, but also know how to apply that knowledge to real-world situations.

Reach out to us for SEO support at hello@truaxmarketing.com and stay tuned for our next blog!
item.excerpt:encoded: There is no one greatest SEO skill, instead, there are many that are important. Today we're going to explore some of the skills you will need to know for 2023.

item.title: Why Managed Hosting is Awesome
item.dc:creator: aarontruax
item.content:encoded:
As a business owner, you understand that having a website or application is essential to success. What you may not realize, however, is that behind-the-scenes infrastructure is just as important. Whether you run an eCommerce site or a development agency with multiple web apps, servers play a crucial role.

More and more businesses are using public cloud providers for their server needs. However, these services can be complex and time-consuming to manage, especially for small businesses with limited resources. This is where we come in.

What is Managed Hosting?

Managed hosting is a kind of web hosting where an entire server is dedicated to host a single website or application. This is in contrast to shared hosting, where one server hosts multiple websites. Although managed hosting is often more expensive, it offers greater personalization, security, and flexibility. 

Managed hosting takes websites to the next level by giving businesses the option to lease dedicated hardware from a service provider, including servers, storage, and networking hardware. Because you have sole use of these resources, it’s referred to as a single-tenant solution. 

As a business owner, you need your website to be available 24/7, but the process of maintaining one yourself and ensuring uptime is costly and time consuming. This is where the magic of managed hosting comes in.

Managed hosting offers a variety of services including OS updates, network infrastructure and hardware monitoring, basic security measures (e.g. virus and spam protection), and more. This way, you can focus on running your business and leave the hosting to the experts. 

Managed hosting is a great solution for anyone who wants to improve their website without investing the extra resources.

The Tradeoffs of Unmanaged Hosting

While managed hosting promises a hassle free experience, unmanaged hosting leaves you responsible for administering the server remotely. This includes, but is not limited to keeping up with software upgrades, patching, security, and monitoring 

Even though you’re spending valuable money renting space from a hosting company, managing your own website is largely a do it yourself experience. Hosting companies are only responsible for maintaining the physical environment of a server, leaving you to fend for yourself when it comes to protecting your investment.

The Consequences of Unmanaged Hosting

Leaving your website in the hands of unmanaged hosts can have disastrous consequences, like your server going down, making your customer base unable to access your website.

While this is a nightmare scenario, it’s far from the last problem that can arise. Other common issues include: 

🔹 Slow-Loading Website 

When your website’s loading speeds are slower than usual, you risk a drop in lead pipelines, resulting in high bounce rates.. 

🔹 Cyber Security Vulnerabilities 

Cyber exploits hit your company hard, causing major problems that could have been avoided had your software been up-to-date with patches.

🔹 Website Crashing

Your new website is getting a lot of attention from the press, but your website cannot handle the traffic and crashes. This causes a lot of inconvenience for your potential customers and reflects poorly on your business.

Benefits of Managed Hosting

As a business owner, the last thing you want to spend your time and money on is server management. That's why fully managed hosting services are the best solution. With these services, you can focus on running your business, while someone else takes care of the server maintenance.

🔹 Reduce Risk

In today’s digital enterprise, risk reduction falls on everyone in an organization. From employees who choose not to open suspicious emails to accountants who ensure financial information is shared securely, protecting your operation is a team effort. 

However, effectively reducing your exposure to online risks is not easy. It takes constant oversight and maintenance to ensure that… 

  • The OS is updated and security patches are applied
  • Cyber security threats are identified and remediated
  • Good traffic is able to flow while suspicious traffic is stopped 

Outsourcing this part of the job to a managed hosting provider is a great way to ensure the job gets done correctly and consistently.

🔹 Keep Up with Business Growth 

As your company grows, it’s essential to have a reliable managed hosting provider who can help you adjust your hosting resources as needed. Good communication with your hosting team is cornerstone to ensuring that your IT infrastructure can support upcoming growth and product launches. 

🔹 Up-To-Date Technology 

As technology continues to develop, many companies are struggling to keep up with the latest advancements. This can result in missed opportunities for improved efficiency and customer service.  

Working with a managed hosting provider allows you to take advantage of the latest technology without having to make a large investment. This way, your company benefits from advances as they happen while your competitors fall behind. 

🔹 Affordable Access to Skilled Experts 

The hosting industry is very familiar with the skills gap. They are able to attract a large number of talented individuals who enjoy the challenge of working with multiple customers. 

Managed hosting providers invest in keeping their team educated and up-to-date on the latest IT threats and opportunities. With every problem they solve for another customer, they are learning new ways to improve your hosting as well.

Why We’re The Best

As the owner of a WordPress site, you know how great of a platform it is. With an array of templates, plugins, and tools available, WordPress makes it possible for businesses large and small to create professional websites. In fact, 43.9% of all internet users rely on WordPress. 

But as with any WordPress site, failing to keep up on essential web maintenance, plugin updates, and cyber security threats will create problems for your operation down the road.

Not to worry – we got you.

With our partner WP Engine FlyWheel we provide the world’s fastest hosting, providing daily back ups, fully managed updated plug-ins, monthly reports, and guaranteed uptimes to keep your website awesome.

Let’s connect hello@truaxmarketing.com.
item.excerpt:encoded: Managed hosting is a type of web hosting in which an entire server is dedicated to a single website or application. This allows for greater control and flexibility than traditional web hosting, and can be more cost-effective.

item.title: The Best LinkedIn Ad Imagery Practices in 2023
item.dc:creator: Wiktoria
item.content:encoded:
As you work to create the perfect ad, you may find yourself wondering what type of imagery performs best on LinkedIn Ads. Though imagery isn’t the most important aspect of your LinkedIn Ads, it’s still something you want to get right.

To help you put together top-performing imagery that is well-suited to the platform, we’ve outlined some best practices below!

🔹 Image Size

Sponsored Content Ads on LinkedIn offer three different options for image size:

  • 1200 x 628 (Horizontal/Landscape)
  • 628 x 1200 (Vertical - Mobile Only)
  • 1200 x 1200 (Square)

Out of the three, we recommend using square imagery for Sponsored Content Ads. Tests have shown that using square imagery resulted in a 15% higher clickthrough rate. 

🔹 Billboard Rule

Your imagery should be concise and to the point. 

People are already reading your ad copy, they don’t want to decipher a lengthy message in your image as well. 

As a best practice, you can use text in your imagery, but keep it short and sweet. 

The billboard rule is perfect - limit yourself to seven words or fewer. 

This will make your ad much more digestible for the reader. 

When creating advertising images for Sponsored Content, keep in mind that there is often not enough space to include a lot of text. 

For other ad formats, such as Text Ads, the imagery is so small that text would not be readable anyway. In these cases, consider using a logo or the offer you are promoting as the subject of the image.

🔹 Image Color

Your image is not the only thing that converts on LinkedIn Ads. 

The purpose of your image is to be an “eye-catcher” - to catch your audience’s attention enough that they stop scrolling and view your message. 

This can often be accomplished through color. 

Use colors in your imagery that stand out against LinkedIn’s color scheme. Avoid colors like blue and white, instead use colors like oranges, greens, yellow, purples and reds. 

For example, this ad stands out because it uses a mix of pink and purple, which contrasts well with the rest of the platform. Over the course of its run, this ad generated thousands of clicks at an average CTR of o.52%. It also generated a total of 2500 leads. 

Though the success of this ad cannot be entirely attributed to its imagery, the image played a pivotal role in capturing the audience’s attention through contrasting colors.

🔹 Image Type

When creating images for LinkedIn Ads, it is important to consider what type of content will perform best.

Will a graphic, photo or text-based image be the most effective?

The key is to keep your target audience in mind when creating imagery, as this captures their attention and makes them want to read your full message. 

Keep your ads professional and tailored to your audience for the best results.

🔹 Conclusion

Your LinkedIn Ads imagery may not be as important as your messaging, but it still plays a key role in driving ROI from your ads. Use these best practices to guide you as you create future LinkedIn Ads imagery.

Do you have questions about LinkedIn Ads? Check out how you can benchmark your LinkedIn Ads here.

Got more questions? Let's connect.
item.excerpt:encoded: LinkedIn Ads are a great way to connect with potential customers and promote your business. So, what type of imagery is most effective in these ads?

item.title: How ChatGPT is a Game-Changer for SEO
item.dc:creator: Wiktoria
item.content:encoded:
ChatGPT is an AI-powered language model by OpenAI that has been making waves in the content creation and marketing industries. This tool uses advanced natural language processing (NLP) algorithms to generate human-like responses to a wide range of queries and prompts. 

ChatGPT has been trained using a massive dataset of text from the internet, which allows it to understand and mimic human language with a high degree of accuracy.

How Does ChatGPT Work?

ChatGPT uses a technique called unsupervised learning, this allows the model to learn patterns and relationships with data without explicit supervision or guidance. The model is trained using a vast dataset of text, and the training process involves adjusting parameters until it can accurately predict the next word or sequence of words in a given piece of text. 

Once the model has been trained, it can be used to generate responses to prompts by predicting the most likely sequence of words based on input. 

Utilizing ChatGPT offers an abundance of advantages for business owners in terms of SEO. Here are a few ways ChatGPT is perfect for content and SEO:

🔹 Generates Unique and Engaging Content

One of the most important factors in SEO is creating unique and engaging content that keeps users on your website. ChatGPT can help you do that just by generating content that is tailored to your specific audience and industry. Whether you need product descriptions, blog posts, or social media content, creating content, ChatGPT can help you create content that keeps your audience coming back for more.

🔹 Provides Topic Clusters

Another important factor in SEO is creating content that is organized into topic clusters. ChatGPT can help you identify key topics and subtopics that are relevant to your audience and niche, allowing you to create content that is both structured and easy to navigate. This helps to improve your SEO rankings by making it easier for search engines to understand the structure and relevance of your content.

🔹 Improves Rankings

Search engines, such as Google, are constantly on the lookout for high-quality content that provides value to users. By using ChatGPT to generate unique and engaging content, you can improve SEO rankings and attract more organic traffic to your website. This is because search engines prioritize content that is relevant, valuable and up-to-date.

🔹 Improves User Experience

ChatGPT can help improve user experience on your website by providing high-quality content that is both informative and engaging. By keeping users on your website longer and providing them with valuable information, you can improve your SEO rankings and attract more organic traffic to your website.

🔹 Saves Time and Effort

Finally, creating high quality content can be a time-consuming and labor-intensive process. With ChatGPT, you can generate content ideas, outlines and even complete articles in a matter of minutes. This can save content creators and marketers a significant amount of time and effort, allowing them to focus on other important things.

Here is an example of ChatGPT generating 10 SEO content ideas : 

We input “Create 10 SEO Content Ideas for Instagram” 

Result : 

ChatGPT also provides the option to ‘regenerate’ its response if you’re unhappy with the results. 

In conclusion, ChatGPT is an excellent tool for content creators and marketers looking to improve their SEO rankings and attract organic traffic to their website. By providing unique and engaging content, improving topic clusters and user experience, ChatGPt can help you create high-quality content that drives results!

Want to perfect your SEO practices? Here are 10 essential SEO skills you need to know in 2023!

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: ChatGPT is an AI-powered language model by OpenAI that has been making waves in the content creation and marketing industries.

item.title: All You Need to Know: Localized SEO
item.dc:creator: Wiktoria
item.content:encoded:
What is Local SEO?

Local SEO, also known as Map SEO, is a specific subset of SEO that is designed to assist small and medium-sized businesses in increasing their online visibility.

It is a crucial aspect of any online business, as it allows them to target local customers effectively. 

In essence, local SEO focuses on optimizing a business's online presence so that it appears in relevant search results when potential customers search for local products or services. For example:

If you operate a hair salon, optimizing your local SEO can help your business show up in search results for “hair salons near me,” making it easier for local customers to find and choose your business. Without proper local SEO, it can be difficult for small businesses to compete in today’s online marketplace.

How SEO Helps Local Businesses

Local businesses used to struggle to compete against national brands online, but with the introduction of Google’s Pigeon update in 2014, local SEO has become a game-changer. Now, local companies can compete for a spot on search result pages on a regional basis. 

It helps businesses connect with their target audience in their vicinity. The best part is that it’s easy to optimize. By linking back to local event sites and incorporating location-specific keywords, businesses can gain a larger online presence based on their region. 

As a result, when nearby visitors search for their products or services, the business’s ranking position will increase, leading to more foot traffic to their store. This is why local businesses are fond of local SEO.

Benefits of Local SEO

🔹 Increased Traffic 

46% of all Google searches are for local information. No matter the size of your business, traffic is essential for success, and local SEO can help enhance it. By focusing on potential customers in your area, you can encourage individuals to visit your website and increase sales. 

🔹 Improved Conversion Rates 

One of the key benefits of local SEO is that it attracts potential customers to your website and improves your conversion rates. By positioning yourself as a local expert in your industry, you increase the chances of becoming the go-to destination for customers looking to make a purchase. Local customers are more likely to prefer buying from businesses they know and trust.

🔹 Reduced Advertising Costs 

Local businesses often spend a significant amount of money on traditional advertising channels like banners, pamphlets and TV ads. But, local SEO can help reduce these costs. Over 80% of smartphone users select the “near me” option when searching for a specific type of business, so optimizing your local SEO can help you appear in these search results and reduce the need of expensive ads.

🔹 Continuous Business Growth 

A comprehensive local SEO strategy focuses on increasing traffic and improving search engine results, leading to more marketing and sales opportunities. As a result, your business can experience continuous growth, with more interested consumers translating into more sales. Investing time and resources into local SEO can be a critical success factor for local business growth.

🔹 Long-Term Relationships 

One of the most significant benefits of local SEO is that the results are long-lasting. Unlike traditional marketing methods, which require constant investment to generate sales, local SEO can provide long-term rankings that will continue to attract customers to your website over time. 

🔹 Competitive Edge

By leveraging the power of local SEO, you can bypass competitors who rely on traditional marketing methods. Customers are more likely to choose a business that appears at the top of search results, so a strong local SEO strategy can help you gain a competitive edge and position your business as a go-to choice for local customers. 

🔹Collaborate with Nearby Businesses for Link Building 

Competition is essential in business, but local SEO can also help you build a relationship with your competitors. By promoting each other's products and services, you can obtain links from nearby businesses. This is a mutually beneficial situation that can increase online visibility and recognition for both companies.

🔹 Optimize Your Site for Mobile Devices 

Mobile devices contribute to many online purchases, and it's essential for businesses to optimize their sites for mobile and tablet devices. The goal of optimization is to simplify the site navigation process, making it easy for users to make purchases. By providing a positive user experience, you can increase the likelihood of customers making a purchase.

🔹 Build a Community 

Local businesses have an advantage in building a strong community. Many people enjoy supporting the local community by shopping locally, which can encourage local business owners to build and strengthen the community. It's helpful to let potential clients know that your business is located nearby, making it easily accessible.

Need help with your local SEO? Let’s connect!

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: #post_excerpt Are you a small or medium-sized business looking to attract more customers in your local area? If so, then Local SEO is the key to achieving that goal.

item.title: Revolutionizing Marketing in 2023: Top Predictions You Need to Know
item.dc:creator: Wiktoria
item.content:encoded:
Welcome to the future of marketing! 

As we embark on a new year, it’s important to take stock of the past and look forward to the trends and innovations that will shape the industry in 2023. 

The world of marketing is constantly evolving, and staying ahead of the curve is essential for businesses to succeed in today’s fast-paced digital landscape. 

Today we’ll explore the key marketing predictions for 2023, so let’s dive in! 

🔹 Brands Shift Away From Third-Party Cookies

In June 2021, Google announced it would be phasing out third-party cookies by late 2023, although this has since been postponed to 2024, and replacing them with a new tracking technology.

As Google chrome is widely used, the advertising industry is faced with the challenge of serving personalized ads and reaching consumers in a post-cookie world. While marketing teams have other digital advertising options available to them, they may need to embrace new technologies to do so.

However, Google is not the first company to eliminate third-party cookies due to privacy concerns.

Apple removed them from Safari and now requires applications to obtain user consent before tracking activity. Mozilla's Firefox also started blocking third-party cookies back in 2019. In addition, privacy-conscious consumers can install extensions to block Google's third-party cookies.

Don’t wait until 2024  to prepare for the change. 

🔹 Increase in Voice Search

Voice search has become increasingly popular in recent years, and its impact on the world of e-commerce is growing rapidly. In fact, sales from voice search were veiled at around 40 billion dollars in the past year, and this figure is projected to increase even further this year.

In order to stay ahead of the curve and take advantage of this trend, businesses need to optimize their content for voice search. 

Here are some tips to help you do just that: 

  • Mobile Friendly Website : Voice search is often used on mobile devices, so it's crucial that your website is optimized for mobile use. Make sure your website loads quickly, and that it's easy to navigate on a small screen.
  • Long-tail Keywords : People tend to use more conversational language when conducting voice searches, so it's important to use long-tail keywords that reflect this. Make sure you integrate these keywords into your content in a way that sounds natural and not forced.
  • Clear and Concise Answers : Many voice searches are phrased in the form of a question, so it's important to provide clear and concise answers to these questions on your website. This will increase the chances of your content being featured in voice search results.
  • Optimize For Conversational Keywords: When optimizing for voice search, it's important to focus on conversational keywords. These are phrases that people use in everyday conversation, and they tend to be longer and more specific than traditional keywords.

🔹Live Commerce will Soar

Live commerce is a rapidly growing trend in the world of e-commerce, particularly in China where it is expected to generate a staggering $387 billion. In live commerce, sellers showcase their products in real-time to an online audience, using live video streaming technology. 

Customers can ask questions and interact with the seller, and make purchases directly within the live stream. Although live commerce has not yet taken off to the same extent in the US as it has in China, it is gaining in popularity. 

This is particularly true when influencers are involved. Influencers are able to use their social media following to attract a large audience to their live streams, and can use their personal brand to promote products in a way that feels more authentic and trustworthy to their followers.

As a result of its success in China, many US businesses are now beginning to explore the potential of live commerce. Some platforms, such as Facebook and Instagram, have even introduced their own live shopping features to capitalize on this trend.

It's clear that live commerce has the potential to revolutionize the world of e-commerce, providing a more engaging and interactive shopping experience for customers, and giving businesses new opportunities to showcase their products and connect with their audience.

🔹 Social Media will Drive E-Commerce

Social media has become an essential tool for businesses to showcase their products and services. According to recent statistics, more than half of consumers use social media to research products before making a purchase. 

Therefore, it's important for businesses to have a strong social media presence and to use it to showcase their products or services effectively.

One way to use social media to showcase products is by posting unboxing videos. These videos can give customers an up-close and personal look at your products, showing them exactly what they can expect when they make a purchase. Unboxing videos can also generate excitement and anticipation for your products.

Another way to use social media to showcase products is by sharing tips on how to use them effectively. This can be particularly effective for complex or technical products that require some explanation. By providing helpful tips and advice on how to use your products, you can show customers that you are committed to providing value and helping them get the most out of their purchase.

Finally, you can use social media to break down how your service works and its outcomes. This can be especially useful for service-based businesses that may not have physical products to showcase. By using social media to explain the ins and outs of your service, you can help potential customers understand what you offer and why they should choose you over the competition.

By using strategies such as unboxing videos, product tips, and service breakdowns, businesses can effectively reach their target audience and increase the chances of making a sale.

🔹 Podcast Advertising will Skyrocket

The popularity of podcasts has grown significantly in recent years, and this trend shows no signs of slowing down. In the UK alone, listeners consume over 60 million hours of podcasts per week, highlighting the significant potential for businesses to reach their target audience through this medium.

One of the key advantages of podcast advertising is that it allows businesses to target a highly engaged audience. Unlike traditional advertising channels, podcast listeners are often highly engaged and interested in the content they are consuming, making them more receptive to advertising messages.

Podcast advertising is more cost-effective when trying to reach a large audience. Compared to traditional advertising channels, such as TV or radio, the cost of producing a podcast and advertising within it can be significantly lower.

Furthermore, research has shown that a whopping 75% of mobile gamers prefer audio ads over video ads. This means that businesses targeting mobile gamers can benefit significantly from advertising on podcasts.

Finally, the potential of podcast advertising is significant, given that there are only 10 million podcasts compared to over a billion blogs. This means that there is still significant room for growth in the podcasting market, and businesses that get involved now can position themselves as early adopters in this growing medium.
item.excerpt:encoded: As we move further into the digital age, marketing continues to evolve at a rapid pace. In 2023, we can expect to see new trends emerge and old ones fade away. Stay ahead of the game with these top marketing predictions for the year ahead.

item.title: The Ultimate Guide to Repurposing Content
item.dc:creator: Wiktoria
item.content:encoded:
As a marketer, your workload can be overwhelming. Leaving you with limited time to complete everything on your to-do list. 

Additionally, you may encounter writer’s block while striving to create fresh and distinctive content. 

While content repurposing might appear to be an attempt to game the system, it is not. Rather, you are taking your existing, superior content and adapting it for alternate platforms in a different format. 

Why Should I Repurpose Content?

Repurposing old content or creating new content with repurposing in mind can significantly save you time. You already possess the data to steer you towards high-performing content pieces that you can restructure and share again. 

If you’re currently developing a blog post that you know will perform well as a podcast episode, a quote snippet on Instagram, or part of an Ebook collection, it would be too good of an opportunity to not repurpose this content.

Moreover, content repurposing can potentially enhance your SEO. When you have various content pieces focused on similar targeted keywords, search engine crawlers will recognize you as an authoritative source. 

By producing valuable resources in multiple formats you’re providing value to your users. 

How to Repurpose Content

🔹 Create an Infographic 

Do you have a blog post full of statistics? 

If so, consider creating an infographic. They are an excellent way to provide consumers with engaging visuals that summarize the most impactful statistics from your posts. You can include these infographics within the blog post or share them on social media platforms. 

🔹 Develop an Ebook

If you have a long-form blog content that offers educational value to your audience, creating an Ebook is a great option. 

In your blog post, you can touch on specific elements of your subject and offer the e-book as a more comprehensive discussion of the content you’ve created.

🔹 Produce a YouTube Video

How-to articles are perfect for repurposing as visual guides on YouTube, particularly for pieces with a lot of graphics. For instance, if your business coaches salespeople on how to ace a sales call, you could write a script and turn it into a blog post or create a YouTube video featuring a real sales person following a script.

🔹 Create a Slideshow

This is one of the simplest methods of repurposing blog content, as you're essentially transforming it into a presentation. Select a piece of high-performing content and recreate it as a Slideshow. They are useful, easily shareable, and they offer a different and more digestible way for audiences to engage with your words. Moreover, presentations enable you to incorporate other graphic elements that may not be present in long-form blog posts.

🔹 Use old images as post background

If you have high-quality visuals that you have only used once, consider using them as post backgrounds on social media. You can use these images as the background for quotes or post the images on their own to draw audience attention to a past project.

🔹 Post snippets of existing video content

If you have YouTube videos or any type of business-related video content, post snippets on social media. Most platforms allow you to post video content, so you can edit it down to fit within their video-length requirements and easily repurpose a video for each platform.

🔹Post user-generated content
